incident.message: Class referenced in the manifest, `com.aiosleeve.aiosleeve.MainActivity$ServiceReceiver`, was not found in the project or the libraries

"agian" is a common misspelling; did you mean "again" ?
Replace "..." with ellipsis character (…, …) ?
Formatting %d followed by words ("hours"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("Hours"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Do not treat position as fixed; only use immediately and call `holder.getAdapterPosition()` to look it up later

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
Do not treat position as fixed; only use immediately and call `holder.getAdapterPosition()` to look it up later
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.

It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

Missing `PendingIntent` mutability flag
Missing `PendingIntent` mutability flag
Missing `PendingIntent` mutability flag
Missing `PendingIntent` mutability flag
Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead
Unnecessary; SDK_INT is always >= 19
Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead
Using `setJavaScriptEnabled` can introduce XSS vulnerabilities into your application, review carefully

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

Provide a timeout when requesting a wakelock with `PowerManager.Wakelock.acquire(long timeout)`. This will ensure the OS will cleanup any wakelocks that last longer than you intend, and will save your user's battery.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
Tag name should use a unique prefix followed by a colon (found My Tag). For instance `myapp:mywakelocktag`. This will help with debugging

Provide a timeout when requesting a wakelock with `PowerManager.Wakelock.acquire(long timeout)`. This will ensure the OS will cleanup any wakelocks that last longer than you intend, and will save your user's battery.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
Do not treat position as fixed; only use immediately and call `holder.getAdapterPosition()` to look it up later

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
This `AsyncTask` class should be static or leaks might occur (com.aiosleeve.aiosleeve.fragments.FragmentECGDetails.MyAsyncTasks)

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

To get local formatting use `getDateInstance()`, `getDateTimeInstance()`, or `getTimeInstance()`, or use `new SimpleDateFormat(String template, Locale locale)` with for example `Locale.US` for ASCII dates.
String literal in `setText` can not be translated. Use Android resources instead.

It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.

Provide a timeout when requesting a wakelock with `PowerManager.Wakelock.acquire(long timeout)`. This will ensure the OS will cleanup any wakelocks that last longer than you intend, and will save your user's battery.

`onTouch` should call `View#performClick` when a click is detected

Custom view ``EditText`` has `setOnTouchListener` called on it but does not override `performClick`

Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background

Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead
Do not treat position as fixed; only use immediately and call `holder.getAdapterPosition()` to look it up later
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.

Action Bar icons should use a single gray color (`#333333` for light themes (with 60%/30% opacity for enabled/disabled), and `#FFFFFF` with opacity 80%/30% for dark themes
Launcher icons should not fill every pixel of their square region; see the design guide for details

The image `icon_device.png` varies significantly in its density-independent (dip) size across the various density versions: drawable-hdpi\\icon_device.png: 12x21 dp (18x31 px), drawable-mdpi\\icon_device.png: 14x22 dp (14x22 px), drawable-xhdpi\\icon_device.png: 13x21 dp (25x42 px), drawable-xxhdpi\\icon_device.png: 12x21 dp (36x62 px)

The image `icon_history.png` varies significantly in its density-independent (dip) size across the various density versions: drawable-hdpi\\icon_history.png: 16x21 dp (24x31 px), drawable-mdpi\\icon_history.png: 18x22 dp (18x22 px), drawable-xhdpi\\icon_history.png: 17x21 dp (33x42 px), drawable-xxhdpi\\icon_history.png: 16x21 dp (49x62 px)

The image `icon_history_arrow.png` varies significantly in its density-independent (dip) size across the various density versions: drawable-hdpi\\icon_history_arrow.png: 8x13 dp (12x20 px), drawable-mdpi\\icon_history_arrow.png: 10x16 dp (10x16 px), drawable-xhdpi\\icon_history_arrow.png: 9x14 dp (17x27 px), drawable-xxhdpi\\icon_history_arrow.png: 8x14 dp (25x41 px)

The image `icon_user.png` varies significantly in its density-independent (dip) size across the various density versions: drawable-hdpi\\icon_user.png: 13x15 dp (20x23 px), drawable-mdpi\\icon_user.png: 19x22 dp (19x22 px), drawable-xhdpi\\icon_user.png: 20x23 dp (39x46 px), drawable-xxhdpi\\icon_user.png: 18x21 dp (53x62 px), drawable-xxxhdpi\\icon_user.png: 15x17 dp (59x69 px)

Cancel button should be on the left (was "Submit | Cancel", should be "Cancel | Submit")
OK button should be on the right (was "OK | Cancel", should be "Cancel | OK")
OK button should be on the right (was "OK | Cancel", should be "Cancel | OK")
